description_long: This is a vintage-style gramophone record player with a large, ornate brass horn featuring decorative perforations and a flared, flower-like shape. The base is octagonal and made of dark polished wood, with carved panels and a sturdy, classic design. A black turntable platter sits atop the base, with a blue label at its center, and a metallic tonearm with a small stylus is positioned over the record. A hand-crank is attached to the side, indicating manual operation, and several small control knobs or switches are visible on the front panel. The overall appearance is elegant and nostalgic, reminiscent of early 20th-century phonographs often found in antique shops or as decorative pieces in urban cafes or vintage-themed venues.
description: A vintage wooden gramophone record player with a large brass horn, black turntable, and hand-crank, featuring ornate details and a classic design.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the ornate brass horn, wooden base, and the turntable platter with the tonearm resting on it.
description_view_1: Direct front view highlighting the control knobs, hand-crank, and the full face of the horn and base.
description_view_2: Right-side view focusing on the profile of the horn, the curve of the tonearm, and the side of the wooden base.
description_view_3: Rear-left view showing the back of the horn, the hand-crank, and the rear panel of the wooden base.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present and centrally located, the turntable platter is circular, black in color, and features a blue label at its center. It appears to be made of plastic or metal, with a smooth, flat surface and no visible grooves or patterns. The platter is proportionally smaller than the base but large enough to accommodate a standard vinyl record.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and slender, extending from the right side of the base toward the center of the platter. It is silver in color with a glossy finish, and appears to be resting on the record, with a visible joint near its base for pivoting.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible as a small, pointed component at the end of the tonearm, appearing metallic and distinct from the rest of the arm. It is attached to the tonearm and positioned over the record.
attribute_control_knobs: Several control knobs are visible on the front panel of the wooden base. They are round, metallic or plastic, and dark in color, with no visible labels or markings. The knobs are small and symmetrically arranged.
